summary: Comprehensive backend pytest suite for ZoomEats. 17/18 tests passed (94%). All core flows working: health, restaurant listing/search/detail, auth (token+cookie), role switching, order creation with subtotal+2.99 fee, customer order retrieval, role guards (403 for cross-role access), vendor restaurant+menu CRUD, delivery accept/deliver flow, admin endpoints (metrics/users/restaurants/orders/approve), AI Chat (Claude Sonnet 4.5 via Emergent LLM key) returning replies and persisting + retrieving history, Stripe checkout session creation. Only failure: GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} returns 500 because emergentintegrations library cannot retrieve the just-created session ('No such checkout.session').

critical.issue: Returns 500. emergentintegrations StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status raises CheckoutError 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...' for a session that was just successfully created via POST /api/checkout/session. Likely the Emergent Stripe proxy issues sessions in a sandbox account but retrieval hits a different account, or sk_test_emergent placeholder is not honored on read. This breaks the post-payment success page polling (GET /checkout/status) flow on the frontend.

action_items: Investigate /api/checkout/status/{session_id} 500 with emergentintegrations StripeCheckout. Either (a) handle CheckoutError gracefully and return a useful payload while session propagates, or (b) verify STRIPE_API_KEY usage — `sk_test_emergent` succeeds on create but fails on status retrieval. Recommend wrapping get_checkout_status in try/except and returning {payment_status: 'pending', status: 'open'} on transient lookup error so the frontend success-page polling does not blow up.
Optional: add input validation to POST /api/orders to reject negative quantities/prices.
Optional: /api/vendor/restaurant returns null (not 404) when no restaurant exists — consider returning 404 for clarity.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 740 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth.py, vendor.py, delivery.py, admin.py, payments.py, chat.py).
POST /api/auth/role allows switching to 'vendor' or 'delivery' but never back to 'customer' check works; however nothing prevents repeated role flipping which could be abused.
create_order does not validate item.price/quantity against the actual menu item — a malicious client can submit arbitrary prices. Recommend re-pricing on the server using db.menu_items.
checkout/status has no try/except around sc.get_checkout_status — any Stripe error causes 500.
stripe_webhook reuses host_url for webhook_url; signature verification relies on emergentintegrations and silently swallows errors.
AI Chat persists messages but does not paginate; chat_history loads up to 200 messages without filter on time.
test_credentials: Test users + sessions are created at runtime via pymongo direct insert into users + user_sessions collections. See backend_test.py make_user(). No real OAuth needed.
seed_data_creation: Seed restaurants (Terra Verde, Hachi Roll Co., Ember & Oak) auto-seeded on backend startup — verified present.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all 18 critical flows. Re-run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL /app/frontend/.env|cut -d= -f2) MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB_NAME=test_database pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Only failing test is test_checkout_session due to Stripe status-retrieval bug. Frontend testing was skipped per review request.
rca of the issue: POST /api/checkout/session succeeds (200) and returns cs_test_xxx session_id with payment_transactions row inserted (payment_status=initiated). Subsequent GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} fails with 500 because emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status raises CheckoutError('Failed to retrieve session status: ... No such checkout.session: cs_test_...'). Same key sk_test_emergent and same webhook_url used in both calls. The session is 'lost' on the read path. Fix: wrap the call in try/except in /api/checkout/status and return a soft-pending response, plus log full Stripe error for further triage; long-term, validate that STRIPE_API_KEY is being correctly resolved by emergentintegrations on both code paths.
